No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to poverty eradication.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course 'Clinical Psychological Assessment' directly addresses mental health and well-being, aligning perfectly with Target 3.4 ('promote mental health and well-being'). This is a core function of clinical psychology.

Quality Education.reason: As a graduate-level course ('Psychology/M/1'), it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops 'Professional competence in psychology' for future employment, aligning with Target 4.4.
evidence: prepare for the future clinical psychology work

Gender Equality.reason: The 'Multiculturalism' core competence implies an awareness of diverse backgrounds, including gender, which can indirectly contribute to ending discrimination (Target 5.1) in assessment practices.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly aims to 'prepare for the future clinical psychology work' and builds 'Professional competence in psychology,' directly contributing to productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5).
evidence: prepare for the future clinical psychology work

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course involves developing 'Research ability' and utilizing 'psychological assessment tools,' which are scientific and contribute to innovation in the field, aligning with Target 9.5.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers 'Intellectual Assessment' and 'Screening Assessment of Cognitive Functions,' vital for identifying and supporting individuals with disabilities. 'Multiculturalism' also promotes inclusion (Target 10.2).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Clinical psychological assessment skills, including 'Case Formulation' and 'Assessment Interview,' are applicable in forensic contexts, contributing to equal access to justice (Target 16.3) and supporting victims (Target 16.2).
